perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean all information disclosed by one Party to the other Party, whether orally, in written, electronic or other format, and whether disclosed by a Party’s agents, principals, employees or representatives, and whether to the other Party's agent's principals, employees or representatives. “Confidential Information” shall include, without limitation, all ideas improvements, inventions, methodologies, works and other innovations of any kind, authored, conceived, developed, made or reduced to practice by the disclosing Party, whether or not eligible for copyright, patent, trademark, trade secret or other legal protection (including, without limitation, formulas, processes, databases, mechanical and electronic hardware, electronic components, computers and their parts, computer programs and their documentation, encoding techniques, marketing and new product plans, production, processes, advertising, packaging and marketing techniques, marketing plans, product plans, technical plans, business strategies, strategic alliances and partners, financial information, engineering data, methodologies and processes, forecasts, personnel information, customer and prospective customer lists, trade secrets, product design, capabilities, specifications, the identify of potential and actual customers, and suppliers and all documentation, materials and media provided by one Party to the other).
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean all information disclosed by one Party to the other Party, whether orally or in writing ONLY, and whether disclosed by a Party’s principals ONLY. “Confidential Information” shall include ONLY the specific trade secrets explicitly marked as such by the disclosing Party in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a narrow definition of Confidential Information, limiting it to information disclosed orally or in writing by principals only and trade secrets explicitly marked as such. This contradicts the original broad definition, particularly if employees share confidential information electronically or if information is not explicitly marked but would reasonably be understood as confidential. This directly contradicts the rest of the section that mentions other means to share the information, introducing an **in-text contradiction**.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Definition of "Confidential Information": The term "Confidential Information" shall mean all information disclosed by one Party to the other Party, whether orally or in writing ONLY, and whether disclosed by a Party’s principals ONLY. “Confidential Information” shall include ONLY the specific trade secrets explicitly marked as such by the disclosing Party in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include orally or written disclosures by principals and explicitly marked trade secrets. The contradicted text is literally the changed text meaning that the modification creates a direct conflict within the document by restricting the scope of what constitutes confidential information, which is a strong contradiction and introduces ambiguity based on the definition provided. This alteration would directly contradict any clause relying on the original, broader definition, introducing an in-text contradiction as described in the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of each Party's disclosure of Confidential Information to the other Party, each Party agrees with respect to the Confidential Information received from the other Party, that it: (a) shall maintain such Confidential Information in the strictest confidence; Pareteum Corporation | 100 Park Ave. | Suite 1600 | New York | NY 10017 | United States Tel. +1 (212) 984.1096 www.Pareteum.com NYSE MKT: TEUM (b) shall not disclose, transfer or otherwise make available any of such Confidential Information to any third party, unless such Confidential Information must be disclosed for the purposes contemplated herein, or under legal compulsion to disclose any such Confidential Information, in which event each Party shall, prior to such disclosure, obtain written consent from the other Party and obtain from the third person a written agreement acknowledging the binding effect of these restrictions regarding the Confidential Information; and (c) shall not directly, indirectly or in concert with any person, use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than evaluating the prospective business relationship with the other Party in accordance with the introduction . Each Party shall take reasonable measures to protect the Confidential Information of the other Party. Those measures shall not be less than the measures taken to protect the receiving Party’s own confidential information. Confidential Information of the other Party may be provided to a Party’s employees only on a need-to-know basis, and prior to such provision, the Party will notify each employee to whom such disclosure is made that such Confidential Information is received in confidence and shall be kept in confidence by such employee.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of each Party's disclosure of Confidential Information to the other Party, each Party agrees with respect to the Confidential Information received from the other Party, that it: (a) shall maintain such Confidential Information in reasonable confidence; (b) shall not disclose, transfer or otherwise make available any of such Confidential Information to any third party, unless such Confidential Information must be disclosed for the purposes contemplated herein. Each Party shall take minimal measures to protect the Confidential Information of the other Party. Confidential Information of the other Party may NOT be provided to a Party’s employees.
perturbation.explanation: By weakening the confidentiality obligation from "strictest confidence" to "reasonable confidence," removing the requirement for written consent for legally compelled disclosures, setting the protection measures to 'minimal measures' and prohibiting it to be provided to a Party's Employees introduces ambiguity. This creates an **in-text contradiction**, especially when considering the potential severity of the legal repercussions for breaching confidentiality as outlined in Section 5. The standard of "reasonable confidence" is less stringent than "strictest confidence", opening the door to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies: If either Party causes a disclosure of the other Party’s Confidential Information in breach of the terms of this Agreement, the disclosing Party shall immediately report in writing the disclosure to the other Party and shall save, defend, indemnify and hold the non-disclosing Party harmless from and against any and all liability and damages suffered by the non-disclosing Party arising therefrom. In addition to the foregoing and without limitation thereof, the disclosing Party shall cooperate in prosecuting any claims against third parties for unauthorized use of any Confidential Information. Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure, use or disposition, whether actual or threatened, of any Confidential Information shall cause irreparable harm, loss of business and significant injury to the disclosing Party, the scope of which would be difficult to ascertain. Each Party agrees, therefore, that the disclosing Party has the right to obtain an immediate injunction against any breach, threatened breach or attempted breach of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedies that may be available at law, including without limitation, the recovery of expenses, costs and attorney’s fees arising out of such breach, threatened breach or attempted breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens confidentiality obligations, stating 'reasonable confidence' instead of 'strictest confidence', imposing 'minimal measures' and prohibiting providing confidential information to a party's employees. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines severe remedies for *any* breach of confidentiality and acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure will cause 'irreparable harm'. The original text requires written consent before legally compelled disclosures, whereas the changed text removes this requirement. These alterations create a strong in-text contradiction because the remedies section assumes a high level of protection and strict adherence to confidentiality, which is undermined by the weakened obligations in the changed text. Thus, the modified text creates a direct conflict with the remedies outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not apply to any information: (a) that has been or which becomes publicly known, through no wrongful act of either Party; (b) which is required to be disclosed in order to comply with applicable law or regulation or with any requirement imposed by judicial or administrative process or any governmental or court order.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not apply to any information: (a) that has been or which becomes publicly known, even through a wrongful act of either Party; (b) which is required to be disclosed in order to comply with applicable law or regulation or with any requirement imposed by judicial or administrative process or any governmental or court order, including but not limited to information related to Securities.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'through no wrongful act' clause and explicitly including 'Securities' this significantly broadens the exceptions to what is considered Confidential Information. This creates an **in-text contradiction** with Section 2, which obligates each party to maintain Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, directly contradicting the intent of the agreement's core confidentiality provision. It also contradicts Section 7, since any information related to 'Securities' now can be exposed, since there is not a penalization against the Party that discloses it. This change makes the overall enforceability uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of "Confidential Information": In consideration of each Party's disclosure of Confidential Information to the other Party, each Party agrees with respect to the Confidential Information received from the other Party, that it: (a) shall maintain such Confidential Information in reasonable confidence; (b) shall not disclose, transfer or otherwise make available any of such Confidential Information to any third party, unless such Confidential Information must be disclosed for the purposes contemplated herein. Each Party shall take minimal measures to protect the Confidential Information of the other Party. Confidential Information of the other Party may NOT be provided to a Party’s employees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the exceptions to what is considered Confidential Information by removing the 'through no wrongful act' clause and including 'Securities'. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which obligates each party to maintain Confidential Information in the strictest confidence and not disclose it to any third party. Now information can be exposed, since there is not a penalization against the Party that discloses it, which directly contradicts the intent of the agreement's core confidentiality provision.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party recognizes that the disclosure of Confidential Information by the disclosing Party shall not be construed as granting any rights, by license or otherwise, concerning any Confidential Information, except as may be explicitly created by this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that the other Party’s Confidential Information includes valuable trade secrets. Neither Party has any obligation to disclose Confidential Information to the other Party. NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY AS TO THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, CONDITION, FITNESS and MERCHANTABILITY, OR PERFORMANCE OF ITS CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party recognizes that the disclosure of Confidential Information by the disclosing Party shall be construed as granting a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the information for any internal purpose. Each Party acknowledges that the other Party’s Confidential Information may or may not include valuable trade secrets. Each Party has an obligation to disclose Confidential Information to the other Party if requested. EACH PARTY MAKES A FULL WARRANTY AS TO THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, CONDITION, FITNESS and MERCHANTABILITY, OR PERFORMANCE OF ITS CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a blatant **in-text contradiction** by stating that disclosure grants a license (contradicting the original no-license clause), questioning whether the information includes trade secrets (contradicting its nature), creating an obligation to disclose (contradicting the no-obligation clause), and giving a full warranty (contradicting the no-warranty clause). This fundamentally alters the nature of the agreement, transforming it from a protective non-disclosure to one where information can be freely used and is guaranteed to be accurate. This affects the balance of power of the agreement, and Section 5 contradicts this section by stating that there should be immediate notification in case of disclosure, when this section is incentivizing disclousure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies: If either Party causes a disclosure of the other Party’s Confidential Information in breach of the terms of this Agreement, the disclosing Party shall immediately report in writing the disclosure to the other Party and shall save, defend, indemnify and hold the non-disclosing Party harmless from and against any and all liability and damages suffered by the non-disclosing Party arising therefrom. In addition to the foregoing and without limitation thereof, the disclosing Party shall cooperate in prosecuting any claims against third parties for unauthorized use of any Confidential Information. Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure, use or disposition, whether actual or threatened, of any Confidential Information shall cause irreparable harm, loss of business and significant injury to the disclosing Party, the scope of which would be difficult to ascertain. Each Party agrees, therefore, that the disclosing Party has the right to obtain an immediate injunction against any breach, threatened breach or attempted breach of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedies that may be available at law, including without limitation, the recovery of expenses, costs and attorney’s fees arising out of such breach, threatened breach or attempted breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions. It grants a license for use, while the contradicted text implies strict confidentiality and consequences for disclosure. The changed text creates an obligation to disclose, directly opposing the original section's protection. The changed text includes a full warranty, while the contradicted text discusses damages for disclosure breaches. These changes create a clear and direct conflict with the confidentiality and liability terms outlined in the contradicted text, incentivizing disclosure while the contradicted text punishes it.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall be returned to the disclosing Parties promptly upon written request or, at the election of the disclosing Party, the Party that received the Confidential Information shall certify said information has been destroyed and is no longer useable in any format.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall be retained indefinitely by the receiving Party, irrespective of any request from the disclosing Party. The receiving Party may use the Confidential Information for any purpose, commercial or otherwise, even after the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The modification states that the receiving party retains the information indefinitely and can use it for any purpose, even after termination. This is a clear **in-text contradiction** with the clause about the term, assignment, and survival (Section 9) where it is explained that 'Each Party’s obligations with respect to the Confidential Information, including but not limited to, sections 2,4,5,6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement and/or return of all Confidential Information from the latter date of either termination or return of such information.', in this situation since it can not be returned it is not viable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term, Assignment and Survival: This Agreement shall be valid unless terminated by mutual written Agreement. Each Party’s obligations with respect to the Confidential Information, including but not limited to, sections 2,4,5,6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement and/or return of all Confidential Information from the latter date of either termination or return of such information. Neither this Agreement nor any rights hereunder, in whole or in part, shall be assignable or otherwise transferable by either Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information, while the changed text states the receiving party retains the information indefinitely, even after termination. This conflicts with the 'Term, Assignment and Survival' section, which states that obligations regarding confidential information survive termination and/or the return of information. Because the modified text states the information can not be returned, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: iPass understands, acknowledges and agrees, AS A MATERIAL INDUCEMENT FOR TEUM TO MAKE AND ENTER INTO THIS AGREEMENT, that with respect to any customer or prospective customer opportunities that are identified by TEUM (collectively, the “ TEUM Leads ”) to iPass, that iPass shall not negotiate directly or indirectly solicit or otherwise attempt to cause the TEUM Leads enter into any form of agreement with iPass without the consent of TEUM, which consent may be given or withheld in TEUM’s sole discretion. In addition to the foregoing, and without limitation thereof, iPass shall not circumvent or otherwise engage in any form of direct or indirect communications with any TEUM Leads without the prior written approval of TEUM, which approval may be given or withheld in TEUM’s sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: iPass understands, acknowledges and agrees that iPass is free to negotiate directly or indirectly solicit or otherwise attempt to cause the TEUM Leads enter into any form of agreement with iPass without the consent of TEUM. iPass is also free to circumvent or otherwise engage in any form of direct or indirect communications with any TEUM Leads without the prior written approval of TEUM.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the restrictions on iPass's ability to negotiate with TEUM Leads and communicate with them directly, this change creates a **in-text contradiction**. The original clause established this restriction as a 'MATERIAL INDUCEMENT,' implying its importance to TEUM entering the agreement. If iPass is now free to solicit TEUM Leads and communicate without approval, the original 'material inducement' is nullified. Making the contract pointless, since it creates a clear contradiction with the introductory paragraph of the contract 'WHEREAS The Parties desire to discuss certain business transactions and to exchange information for the purpose of exploring a potential business relationship for the benefit of the Parties and/or to sign a business contract that shall include confidential technical or business information of each Party or entitle each Party to exchange information for the execution of this business contract.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS The Parties desire to discuss certain business transactions and to exchange information for the purpose of exploring a potential business relationship for the benefit of the Parties and/or to sign a business contract that shall include confidential technical or business information of each Party or entitle each Party to exchange information for the execution of this business contract. In order to facilitate these discussions and in order for the Parties to receive from each other, either orally or in writing, certain technical and business information under terms that will protect the confidential and proprietary nature of such information, the Parties have entered into this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly restricts iPass from negotiating with or communicating with TEUM Leads without TEUM's consent, establishing this as a 'MATERIAL INDUCEMENT'. The changed text removes these restrictions, allowing iPass to freely negotiate and communicate with TEUM Leads. This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause in the contradicted text, which states the parties' desire to explore a potential business relationship based on exchanging information under terms that protect confidential information. By allowing iPass to freely engage with TEUM Leads, the change undermines the very foundation of the agreement and the need to protect confidential information, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.
